worked: The supervisor-pre-vote-nomination ritual surfaced its own meta-flaw via jester (audit-trail comparison was the actual structural defense, not the ritual itself). The self-critique meta-loop genuinely improved the system.
Cheap Sonnet test battery validated jester-disruption hypothesis empirically — partial validation + honest pushback better than speculation.

Iterative user calibration (jester quality gate, diversity policy, lite-as-default) was load-bearing — design doc shifted significantly each turn based on real feedback. Don't ship without that loop for non-trivial features.
Filing proposals as I go (24 opened, all closed by end) kept the followup queue tractable instead of remembering threads.
didnt_work: Bash sandbox-mangled JSON heredoc test infrastructure (markdown-fenced voter-1 test file ended up as valid JSON). Test infrastructure has its own bugs; don't infer system bugs from test setup bugs.
Initial mcp-catalog audit (earlier in thread) used wrong JSON key — self-corrected, but illustrates: when jq says "no result," verify the key name before concluding the file is broken.
Bash tool subprocess sandbox blocks `find /tmp` traversal — startup-script dry-run from inside Claude reports 0 files even when there ARE files. Real execution outside sandbox works correctly.

My pre-vote nomination of voter-3 vs panel's unanimous voter-4 — builder's-investment bias toward "actionable bugs I can fix today" over "literature undercut that changes design." Both valid; panel's pick was the more durable.

Tab-title prepend bug: idempotency is easy to forget when adding a prefix to a value that becomes input to the next iteration of the same function. Always check "would this run twice without breakage?"
notes: MAGI default IS lite mode. Don't surprise the user with a $2 run by reaching for full mode silently. Auto-escalation hint requires user confirm.
The retry-on-malformed loop (Phase 6.2) needs first-real-wild-fire to validate end-to-end. Until then, assume the unit-level aggregator behavior is what we know works.
Cost-capture parses `<usage>` from Agent tool output text — there's a documented schema in skill but not unit-tested yet. First real run is the test.
The supervisor self-bias matrix is INSTRUMENTATION, not enforcement. High self-bias (Δ > 1.5) requires supervisor to cite specific reasoning when ruling for that voter — NOT to drop their vote.

`assets/magi/<YYYYMMDD-HHMM-slug>/` is the archive root. Never moves; one dir per run. If you see hundreds of dirs accumulate, that's expected — write a `assets/magi/INDEX.md` regenerable view (file as proposal if user asks).

The "jester unnecessarily disruptive" hypothesis is PARTIALLY validated. On routine tool-compare questions: yes, marginal. On consequential design / "should we" / rewrite: jester earns its keep. Auto-on heuristic now skips tool-compare patterns.
`personas/_proposed/` is where MAGI drafts new personas inline. After 3+ reuses, graduate to `~/.claude/personas/` proper — future automation.
feedback: "Intelligent disobedience > sycophancy > lazy work-skipping" — confirmed as load-bearing operational preference. Affirm event captured + flows to dreaming.
"We want SOME diversity, not a typical MUN summit" — diversity policy default to same-model with knobs for variance; cross-family opt-in only via --diverse.
"Pessimists right, optimists get things done" — partially validated as why jester defaults to off for routine; partially pushed-back where jester earns its keep on consequential decisions.
"Maintaining workspace + TodoWrite + memory redundantly is intentional, sync is a chore" — captured as memory entry; sync protocol filed as proposal (not yet built).
"Cost should be shown EVERY time, %-weekly limit, per-step breakdown" — landed in Phase 11 mandatory end-of-run cost block.
